fragility_points: Dependence on extracted page-source rules rather than a separately rendered rules panel capture.
Lack of archived Binance 1-minute candle evidence in the artifact set.
key_assumptions: The embedded Polymarket rules text extracted from page source is operative for this outcome.
The resolved/closed state shown in page source is current and not a stale frontend artifact.
Later sub-$2,400 trading does not matter once a qualifying Binance 1-minute high already occurred.
main_logical_chain: Read the contract as a narrow venue-specific threshold-touch market rather than a broad week-ahead ETH directional market.
Verify the governing source of truth from Polymarket embedded rules: Binance ETH/USDT 1-minute Highs during the ET date window.
Check whether the relevant outcome appears already settled on the market page source.
Conclude that the decisive catalyst already occurred, so Yes is effectively the answer unless the rules/state were misread.
main_thesis: The market is effectively already decided because the governing catalyst was a Binance ETH/USDT 1-minute touch above $2,400, and the Polymarket page source shows the ↑ 2,400 leg already resolved Yes.
quote_anchors: This market will immediately resolve to Yes if any Binance 1-minute candle for ETH/USDT... has a final High price equal to or greater than the price specified in the title.
Prices from other exchanges, different trading pairs, or spot markets will not be considered for the resolution of this market.
source_quality_view: Primary evidence quality is high for contract interpretation but somewhat concentrated on Polymarket page source; independent confirmation is moderate and mainly contextual.
strongest_disconfirmers: I did not independently retrieve the exact Binance historical 1-minute candle that crossed $2,400.
If the page-source resolution state were stale or malformed, confidence would be too high.
strongest_supports: Polymarket embedded rules specify Binance ETH/USDT 1-minute Highs as the sole resolution source.
The ↑ 2,400 leg showed outcomePrices [1,0], closed true, and umaResolutionStatus resolved in page source.
Kraken showed a same-day ETH/USD high above $2,400, making a Binance threshold breach plausible.
timing_relevance: Timing mattered almost entirely through an already-realized threshold-touch catalyst; once that happened, the rest of the week became much less relevant.
unresolved_ambiguities: Exact timestamp of the qualifying Binance 1-minute high was not preserved in the notes.
Whether a later auditor will want direct Binance candle export for maximal provenance.
what_would_change_view: Direct Binance 1-minute historical data showing no qualifying high, or evidence that the Polymarket resolved state/rules were misread, would materially change the view.
